title: Glowing Grilled Venezuelan Fish Ceviche
description: A refreshing, energetic Venezuelan dish featuring a delicate balance of fish, citrus, and spices.

ingredients.name: Fresh tilapia fillets (4)
ingredients.notes: Freshness is key for this recipe

ingredients.name: Jalapeño pepper (seeded and finely chopped) (1/2 cup)

instructions: Rinse the fish under cold water, pat dry with a paper towel.
In a large bowl, combine lime juice, nam pla, cumin, cilantro, red onion, jalapeño pepper, and garlic.
Add the fish to the marinade, cover the bowl with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat the broiler to high heat, about 450°F (230°C).
Remove the fish from the marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off.
Place the fish on a broiler pan, skin side up (if it has skin).
Broil the fish for 4-6 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Remove the fish from the broiler, and let it cool slightly.
Cut the fish into small pieces, and add it to the marinade.
Stir to combine, and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for at least 10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Serve the ceviche immediately, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
